info.title: SEPM COMMAND-QUEUE API
info.description: Self-contained OpenAPI 2.0 specification for SEPM command-queue endpoints.

parameters.name: computer_ids
parameters.description: The list of computers on which to run the command.

parameters.name: group_ids
parameters.description: The list of groups on which to run the command.

parameters.name: power
parameters.description: Specify whether to run an aggressive scan.

200.description: The web service successfully processed the web request and returned a result.

401.description: The user that is currently logged on has insufficient rights to execute the web method, or the user is unauthorized.

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

500.description: The web service encountered an error while processing the web request.

post.summary: Sends a command from Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager to Symantec Endpoint Protection endpoints to request an active scan on the endpoint. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.name: computer_ids
parameters.description: The list of computer IDs from which to request the baseline information.

parameters.name: group_ids
parameters.description: The list of group IDs from which to request the baseline information.

200.description: The web service successfully processed the web request and returned a result.

401.description: The user that is currently logged on has insufficient rights to execute the web method, or the user is unauthorized.

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

500.description: The web service encountered an error while processing the web request.

post.summary: Sends a command from Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager to Symantec Endpoint Protection endpoints to request that baseline application information be uploaded back to Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.description: The target groups or computers to which this command should be applied.

post.summary: Sends a command from Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager to Symantec Endpoint Protection endpoints to request that those endpoints communicate directly with the cloud instead of Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

get.summary: Gets the binary file content for a given file ID. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: The file ID from which to get detailed information.

200.description: The web service successfully processed the web request and returned a result.

get.summary: Gets the details of a binary file, such as the checksum and the file size. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.name: computer_ids
parameters.description: The list of computers on which to search for the suspicious file.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: The file path of the suspicious file.

parameters.name: md5
parameters.description: The MD5 hash value of the suspicious file.

parameters.name: sha1
parameters.description: The SHA1 hash value of the suspicious file.

parameters.name: sha256
parameters.description: The SHA256 hash value of the suspicious file.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source to search for the suspicious file. Possible values are: FILESYSTEM (default), QUARANTINE, or BOTH. 12.1.x clients only use FILESYSTEM.

200.description: The web service successfully processed the web request and returned a result.

401.description: The user that is currently logged on has insufficient rights to execute the web method, or the user is unauthorized.

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

500.description: The web service encountered an error while processing the web request.

post.summary: Sends a command from Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager to Symantec Endpoint Protection endpoints to request a suspicious file be uploaded back to Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.description: Specify reboot options for an aggressive power scan on agents older than 14.3 RU5 or default options are used.

post.summary: Sends a command from Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager to Symantec Endpoint Protection endpoints to invalidate IRON cache entries on the endpoint. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.description: the license policy to apply to the selected groups or computers.

post.summary: Sends a command from Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager to Symantec Endpoint Protection endpoints to override the default license policy. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

post.summary: Sends a command from Symantec Endpoint Protection Manager to Symantec Endpoint Protection endpoints to reset license policy to default instance. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

post.summary: Sends a command from SEPM to SEP endpoints to update content. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.name: command_id
parameters.description: The command ID for which details are needed.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Specifies whether the results are in ascending order (ASC) or descending order (DESC).

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: The index page for returned results. The default page index is 1.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of results to include on each page. The default is 20.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The column by which the results are sorted. The default is by command's start time.

200.description: The web service successfully processed the web request and returned a result.

401.description: The user that is currently logged on has insufficient rights to execute the web method, or the user is unauthorized.

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

500.description: The web service encountered an error while processing the web request.

get.summary: Gets the details of a command status. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.

parameters.name: command_id
parameters.description: The command ID to be canceled.

200.description: The web service successfully processed the web request and returned a result.

401.description: The user that is currently logged on has insufficient rights to execute the web method, or the user is unauthorized.

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

500.description: The web service encountered an error while processing the web request.

post.summary: Cancels an existing command by creating a new cancel command for clients for which the command is still pending. A system administrator account is required for this REST API.
